mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 compared to single predictors.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and force rapid equity repricing downward within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of contraction periods, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy when industrial production growth exceeds 1.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate creates real rate compression that forces markets to price in imminent tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields positive returns in 65% of observations, or the combined signal fails to achieve a monthly out-of-sample hit rate above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports long-term corporate revenue projections, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in 60% of high-growth payroll months, or the correlation between payroll growth and quarterly equity returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction below zero percent triggers immediate recession fears that override short-term liquidity support, causing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in 58% of contraction quarters, or the signal fails to predict direction when GDP declines are accompanied by central bank rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in 62% of cases, or predictive accuracy vanishes when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.
